summary:
The presence of gram-negative diplococci in pus cells and growth on Thayer-Martin medium confirms *Neisseria gonorrhoeeae* as the pathogen responsible for this case. Recurrent infection due to antigenic variation allows evasion of adaptive immunity by altering surface proteins, leading to repeated bouts of disease even after initial treatment.

Final Answer: B
Only include text inside boxed content or directly below it.

\boxed{B}  
The presence of gram-negative diplococci in white blood cells and growth on Thayer-Martin agar indicates *Neisseria gonorrhoeae*. Recurrence results from antigenic variation that enables immune escape via altered surface antigens.
